nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont was born about 1260, in Witnesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom as the son of lord john de beaumont and lady catherine de everingham. He had at least 1 daughter with Alice de Beaumont. He died after 1289, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont est né environ 1260, à Witnesham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de lord john de beaumont et lady catherine de everingham. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Alice de Beaumont. Il est décédé après 1289, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont wurde um 1260 in Witnesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von lord john de beaumont und lady catherine de everingham geboren. Er hatte mit Alice de Beaumont mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1289 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont è nato il circa 1260 nel Witnesham, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di lord john de beaumont e lady catherine de everingham. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Alice de Beaumont. Morì dopo il 1289, nel sua cittadina.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont(은)는 대략 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 윗네샤에서 lord john de beaumont(와)과 lady catherine de everingham의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Alice de Beaumont(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1289년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont nasceu aproximadamente 1260, em Witnesham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de lord john de beaumont e lady catherine de everingham. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Alice de Beaumont. Ele faleceu após 1289, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Beaumont родился около 1260 г. в местности витнешам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали lord john de beaumont и lady catherine de everingham. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Alice de Beaumont. Он умер после 1289 г. в его родном городе.
